notable_pick_trades: Traded 3rd-round pick (No. 92) to Cowboys for DT Osa Odighizuwa. Previously traded 5th-rounder to Eagles for Bryce Huff (who has since retired), 6th to Commanders for RB Brian Robinson, 7th to Texans for DT Khalil Davis. Received three compensatory 4th-round picks (133, 138, 139). Net: 6 picks remaining.
roster_context: San Francisco went 12-5 in 2025 and won a Wild Card game before a 41-6 Divisional Round loss to Seattle exposed depth issues. The roster was ravaged by injuries: Nick Bosa (ACL), Mykel Williams (ACL), George Kittle (Achilles in playoffs), Fred Warner (broken ankle). The 2025 offseason purge of Greenlaw, Ward, Hufanga, Banks, and Floyd created holes the team is still filling. New DC Raheem Morris replaces Robert Saleh (now Titans HC). The 49ers are in win-now mode around Brock Purdy's prime but need significant roster replenishment, especially on the edges and at WR.

needs.explanation: The 49ers recorded a league-low 20 sacks in 2025 — the NFL's worst by a wide margin. Nick Bosa and 2025 1st-rounder Mykel Williams are both returning from torn ACLs with uncertain timelines. Bryce Huff retired. Beyond Bosa and Williams, the cupboard is bare. Keion White is a rotational piece acquired mid-season from New England. The team pursued John Franklin-Myers in free agency but was outbid by Tennessee. Joey Bosa remains a free agent target but hasn't signed yet. Even if Bosa and Williams return healthy, the 49ers desperately need edge depth and a legitimate No. 2 rusher.
needs.current_starter: Nick Bosa (returning from ACL), Mykel Williams (returning from ACL)
needs.depth: Keion White (rotational), Sam Okuayinonu (tendered RFA). Bryce Huff retired. No proven No. 2 pass rusher on roster.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 — this is the most commonly mocked position at pick 27

needs.explanation: Brandon Aiyuk missed all of 2025 and is being released/traded. Jauan Jennings is an unsigned free agent who likely priced himself out. Kendrick Bourne signed with Arizona. Skyy Moore signed with Green Bay. The 49ers signed Mike Evans (3 years, $60.4M) but he is 32 and coming off an injury-limited 8-game 2025 season. Behind Evans, the room is Ricky Pearsall (injury-prone, 20 games in 2 years), Demarcus Robinson, Jacob Cowing (hamstring issues), and Jordan Watkins. George Kittle's Achilles tear further reduces passing weapons. This position still needs a young, dynamic WR2 via the draft.
needs.depth: Demarcus Robinson, Jacob Cowing, Jordan Watkins — very thin and unproven behind Evans/Pearsall
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — multiple mock drafts project WR at 27 or in Round 2

needs.explanation: Trent Williams will turn 38 in July and is embroiled in a contract standoff with a $39M cap hit. While a restructured deal is the most likely outcome, the 49ers have no viable successor. Vederian Lowe was signed as a swing tackle but is a backup, not a starter. Long-term, the 49ers must find Williams' replacement — multiple mock drafts project an OT at pick 27. Even if Williams returns for 2026, drafting his heir is prudent. RT Colton McKivitz is solid and signed through 2028.
needs.current_starter: Trent Williams (LT, contract dispute), Colton McKivitz (RT)
needs.depth: Vederian Lowe (signed, swing tackle), Austen Pleasants. No in-house LT successor.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — developmental LT to learn behind Williams

needs.explanation: Left guard is a clear hole. Spencer Burford ranked as the worst starting LG in pass protection per PFF in 2025 (48.5 grade). Ben Bartch couldn't stay healthy. Both are now free agents. The 49ers signed Brett Toth (one-year deal), but he's best at center and is a depth piece. Center Jake Brendel (34) and RG Dominick Puni are adequate starters, but LG needs a clear upgrade. The draft's guard class (Pregnon, Ioane, Proctor) offers options.
needs.current_starter: LG: TBD (Burford/Bartch both UFAs), C: Jake Brendel, RG: Dominick Puni
needs.depth: Brett Toth (signed, interior swing), Nick Zakelj (re-signed). LG is an open competition.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — could address at 27 if a top IOL/OT falls, or Round 2 at 58

needs.explanation: The safety room lacks a true single-high free safety. Malik Mustapha (SS) had a strong 2025 return from ACL surgery (76 tackles, 12 starts) and is the best player in the group. Ji'Ayir Brown has been erratic and graded poorly in coverage (62.5 PFF coverage grade in single-high). Jason Pinnock re-signed with the Giants. Richie Grant is gone. Marques Sigle (2025 5th-rounder) is unproven. The scheme under Raheem Morris will demand more Cover 3, requiring a rangy FS — neither Mustapha nor Brown fits that mold well.
needs.depth: Marques Sigle (2nd-year), Siran Neal. Pinnock departed to Giants. Thin and lacking playmaking.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4 — strong 2026 safety class makes this addressable on Day 2 or with comp picks

needs.explanation: Deommodore Lenoir is a solid CB1. Renardo Green showed talent but was inconsistent and fell out of favor. 2025 3rd-rounder Upton Stout developed well as the nickel. The 49ers added Nate Hobbs on a 1-year deal from Green Bay, giving slot competition. The room has young pieces but lacks a proven, consistent CB2 opposite Lenoir. Lost Charvarius Ward last offseason. Could use another outside corner but not a top-2 priority given Hobbs addition.
needs.current_starter: Deommodore Lenoir (outside), Renardo Green (outside), Upton Stout (nickel)
needs.depth: Nate Hobbs (signed, slot/nickel competition), Siran Neal, Darrell Luter Jr. Improved with Hobbs but still developing.

needs.explanation: The trade for Osa Odighizuwa (from Dallas for a 3rd-round pick) was a significant move to address interior pass rush. The 2025 rookies Alfred Collins and CJ West improved the run defense but provided little pass rush. Jordan Elliott signed with Tennessee. Kalia Davis signed with Cleveland. Odighizuwa should be the interior pass-rush presence they lacked, but additional depth is still needed, especially with the team's overall D-line health concerns.
needs.current_starter: Osa Odighizuwa (acquired via trade), Alfred Collins, CJ West
needs.depth: Sam Okuayinonu (tendered RFA). Improved with Odighizuwa trade but depth is thin.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 4 (comp picks) or later — addressed via trade but could add depth

needs.explanation: George Kittle tore his Achilles in the Wild Card game (Jan 11) and is targeting a return 'well before November' but realistically may miss the first several weeks. He turns 33 in October. Jake Tonges was re-signed (2-year deal) and filled in admirably, but the 49ers need insurance and a long-term succession plan given Kittle's age and injury history. Could be addressed in mid-rounds.
needs.current_starter: George Kittle (Achilles rehab, may miss early season)
needs.depth: Jake Tonges (re-signed), Hayden Rucci. Thin if Kittle misses time early.

free_agency_impact: Signed WR Mike Evans (3 years, $60.4M) — addresses WR1 void from Aiyuk/Jennings departures, but still need a young WR2
Traded for DT Osa Odighizuwa from Cowboys (cost: 3rd-round pick) — significantly upgrades interior D-line pass rush
Signed LB Dre Greenlaw (1-year reunion deal) — fills LB2 need alongside Fred Warner, removes LB from critical needs
Signed OT Vederian Lowe (2 years, $12M) — swing tackle depth, not a Williams replacement
Signed OL Brett Toth (1-year deal) — interior OL depth, could compete at LG
Signed CB Nate Hobbs (1-year, up to $4.5M) — adds slot/nickel competition with Upton Stout
Re-signed LB Luke Gifford (2 years, $5.3M) — Pro Bowl special teamer returns
Re-signed K Eddy Piñeiro (4-year extension) — kicker position settled
Re-signed TE Jake Tonges (2-year deal) — Kittle insurance while he recovers from Achilles
Bryce Huff retired — increases EDGE need substantially, removes the nominal No. 2 DE from roster
Lost WR Kendrick Bourne (signed with Cardinals, 2 years/$12M) — further thins WR room
Lost WR Skyy Moore (signed with Packers) — lost KR/PR specialist
Lost DT Jordan Elliott (signed with Titans) — D-line depth departed, partly offset by Odighizuwa trade
Lost DT Kalia Davis (signed with Browns) — another interior DL depth loss
Lost S Jason Pinnock (re-signed with Giants) — safety depth loss
Lost DE Robert Beal Jr. (signed with Dolphins) — edge depth departed
Brandon Aiyuk expected to be released/traded — WR room in complete flux
Jauan Jennings still unsigned as free agent — likely departing, further depletes WR room
Trent Williams contract standoff ongoing ($39M cap hit) — not expected to be traded per Schefter, but restructure needed
scheme_fit_notes: New DC Raheem Morris brings a versatile scheme background. Expect more Cover 3 with a single-high safety, elevating the need for a rangy FS. Morris has his edge rushers stand up at times, so versatile EDGE prospects who can rush from 2-point and 3-point stances have added value. Kyle Shanahan's wide-zone offense demands athletic OL who can move laterally, making pure power blockers less ideal at guard. WR prospects with RAC ability and route-running nuance fit the Shanahan scheme — think versatile playmakers, not pure deep threats.
